original_question: My wheat plants are looking weak and some are drying up, what's going on?

My wheat plants are looking weak and some are drying up, what's going on?

It sounds like termites might be the problem. They attack the roots and lower stems of wheat plants, causing yellowing, stunted growth, and plants that pull out easily. You can try treating the seeds before sowing with Chlorpyriphos 20% EC or thiomethoxam 30% FS. Also, make sure to keep the field clean and use well-decomposed manure. If the problem is already there, you can use Chlorpyrifos 20% EC with irrigation water. Be careful when using chemicals and follow the instructions closely.

Apply Chlorpyriphos 20% EC as a seed treatment before sowing, following the recommended dosage. If termites are already present, dilute the same product in irrigation water and apply it to the field.

Apply Chlorpyriphos 20% EC as a seed treatment before sowing. If termites are already present, dilute the product in irrigation water and apply it to the field during the growing season.
What preventive measures can I take to avoid termite infestation in my wheat field?
Keep the field clean and use well-decomposed manure to prevent termite infestation.

original_question: My paddy field has a lot of wild onion, what can I do about it?

messages.content: You are an agricultural advisory assistant helping farmers with crop management.
My paddy field has a lot of wild onion, what can I do about it?

You can control wild onion in your paddy by using herbicides. Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC at 600 ml per acre or Pyrazosulfuron ethyl 10% WP at 60 gm per acre within 3 days after transplanting. For later control, use Bispyribac sodium 10% SC at 100 ml per acre at 20-25 days after transplanting. Also, hand weeding at 20-25 and 40-45 days after transplanting works well. Keep 2-3 inches of water in the field when applying the chemicals.
How many times should I perform hand weeding for wild onion control?
Hand weeding should be done at 20-25 and 40-45 days after transplanting.

Apply the herbicides by spraying them evenly over the field while maintaining 2-3 inches of water in the field. Ensure proper coverage and follow the specified dosages for each chemical.

Apply Pretilachlor 50% EC within 3 days after transplanting and Bispyribac sodium 10% SC at 20-25 days after transplanting.

The yellowing of lower leaves in your pea plants is a sign of wilt disease. This happens because of root rot caused by fungi like Fusariumoxysporum and Rhizoctonia solani. If you're planting peas for the first time in an area, treat the seeds with Rhizobiumleguminosarum to help them grow better.

My paddy field has salty soil, what can I do to fix it?

To fix salty soil in your paddy field, add organic matter like paddy or wheat straw. Mix it into the soil up to 15–20 cm deep in April or May. Water it 2–3 times to help break down the residues and wash away salts. Also, plow deep to improve drainage and use green manure like dhaincha before planting. If the soil is very salty, use gypsum as recommended by a soil test. Keep adding organic matter over the years and choose salt-tolerant paddy varieties.

At planting time, sugarcane needs a good mix of nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium. A general recommendation is 60 kg of nitrogen, which is about 130 kg of urea, for planting. If the soil is low in phosphorus and potassium, add 12 kg of phosphorus and 30 kg of potassium per acre. Apply all the phosphorus and potassium right at planting. For ratoon crops, give one-third of the nitrogen in February. If the soil is dry, apply half the nitrogen at planting and the rest when it rains. To deal with armyworm and silk cutter, spray the crop with chlorantraniliprole 18.5 SC or emamectin benzoate 5 SG. original_question: My wheat plants are getting yellow leaves, what's causing it and how can I fix it?

messages.content: You are an agricultural advisory assistant helping farmers with crop management.
My wheat plants are getting yellow leaves, what's causing it and how can I fix it?

Yellow leaves in wheat, also called 'panda,' are usually because the soil lacks zinc, iron, or nitrogen. This happens a lot in areas like Kaushambi in Uttar Pradesh, where the soil is alkaline and doesn’t release nutrients properly. If the leaves are yellow with green veins, it's likely zinc deficiency. If the new leaves are uniformly yellow, it might be iron. If older leaves are pale, it's probably nitrogen. To fix it, spray 2 kg zinc sulphate and 6 kg urea in 320 liters of water per acre. If you already used urea, mix 1 kg zinc sulphate with 1 kg lime water instead. Apply 10 kg zinc sulphate per acre with farmyard manure before sowing. Make sure to use the right fertilizers and keep the soil pH balanced. If the yellowing doesn’t go away, check for other problems like disease or water issues.

Apply 10 kg zinc sulphate per acre with farmyard manure before sowing. For foliar spray, use 2 kg zinc sulphate and 6 kg urea in 320 liters of water per acre during the growing season.

What is the correct method to cut maize for fodder?
Cut your maize when it's in the milk ripe stage or dough stage. Don't take the cobs off if you're using it as fodder. Make sure you harvest before the grains get too hard.

My maize plants are getting too tall and the leaves are turning brown, when should I cut them for the best animal feed?

Cut your maize when it's in the milk ripe stage or dough stage. This is when the plant has the best nutrition for animals. Don't take the cobs off. Keep the plants as silage if you have extra. The time to cut depends on the variety you're growing.
